title: Malaysian Chicken Satay
description: A classic Southeast Asian dish featuring marinated chicken grilled to perfection and served with a side of spicy peanut sauce.
instructions: In a blender or food processor, combine coconut milk, peanut butter, soy sauce, palm sugar, garlic, galangal, lemongrass, and satay spice mix. Blend until smooth.
Place the chicken breast in a large bowl and pour the marinade over it. Mix well to coat the chicken evenly.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat.
Thread the marinated chicken onto skewers, leaving a little space between each piece.
Brush the grill with vegetable oil to prevent sticking.
Grill the chicken for 5-7 minutes per side, or until cooked through.
Serve the chicken satay hot with a side of spicy peanut sauce.
To make the peanut sauce, combine peanut butter, soy sauce, palm sugar, and water in a blender or food processor. Blend until smooth.
Add chili flakes to taste and blend until well combined.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
